title: Eugenia leptalea
taxonomy: Eugenia leptalea is classified as a species of plant in the family Myrtaceae. This family is commonly known as the myrtle family and consists of around 150 genera and over 6,000 species. The Myrtaceae family is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world, with the majority of species found in Australia and Southeast Asia. Eugenia leptalea is native to Brazil, and its classification further breaks down to the order Myrtales, and the clade Rosids. The specific classification of Eugenia leptalea is still under review and may be subject to change as new research becomes available.
